channel.title: nexusEVnews
channel.description: Independent EV and renewable energy news, curated daily. Electric vehicles, solar, battery tech, and charging infrastructure.

item.title: The Hyundai Ioniq 9 Just Beat Four Expensive Rivals at Their Own Game
item.dc:creator: carwow
item.description: carwow's Matt Watson lines up the BMW iX, Kia EV9, Hyundai Ioniq 9, Polestar 3, and Volvo EX90 for a full group test covering design, real-world range, interior quality, driving, boot space, and a drag race. Estimated real-world ranges run from 202 miles for the Kia EV9 to 317 miles for the Polestar 3. The BMW iX wins the drag race at 4.4 seconds to 60 mph with 544 horsepower. The Polestar 3 is the pick if you only need five seats. For the full seven-seat test, the Ioniq 9 edges the Kia EV9 on practicality, interior quality, and value.

item.title: The Slate Truck Leaves the Factory in One Color. The Designer Planned It That Way.
item.dc:creator: Javier Mota
item.description: Slate Auto head of design Tisha Johnson explains the philosophy behind the Slate pickup: affordable at purchase, affordable to own, and affordable to personalize. The truck leaves the factory in a single slate gray and is the first vehicle designed specifically to be DIY-wrapped. Over 100 wrap colors are planned. The dashboard can swap between a standard instrument panel and a full sound bar. The two-seater converts to a five-seat SUV by popping out the rear bulkhead and installing a bench seat. Reservations are open, with deliveries targeted for later this year.

item.title: Rivian R2 EPA Docs Are Out. The Range Came In Better Than Rivian Said It Would.
item.dc:creator: RivianTrackr
item.description: EPA certification documents for the Rivian R2 have surfaced ahead of customer deliveries. The Performance trim on 21-inch All-Season tires earned 335 miles, five miles better than Rivian's own estimate of 330. The 20-inch All-Terrain version came in at 314 miles, also beating Rivian's 307-mile projection. Usable battery is 86.8 kWh and DC fast charging peaks at 210 kW. Payload capacity is roughly 1,175 lbs. The repositioned heat pump is confirmed to ship from day one, including on the Launch Edition. Employee deliveries are underway this month.

item.title: Four People Who Had Never Driven an EV Took the MG4 Urban Out for a Day. Here Is What They Said.
item.dc:creator: Electrifying
item.description: Electrifying recruits four real-world drivers, none of them EV owners, to spend a day with the MG4 Urban. The car offers around 258 miles of range and charges from 10 to 80% in about 30 minutes on a rapid charger. Highlights include five levels of regenerative braking, a one-pedal mode that brings the car to a full stop without touching the brake, 30 storage areas built throughout the cabin, and a boot floor that lowers to nearly double the usable depth. All four left saying it was easier and more practical than expected.

item.title: Tesla FSD 14.3 Is Out. The AI Compiler Was Rebuilt, and Reaction Time Is 20% Faster.
item.dc:creator: Dirty Tesla
item.description: Dirty Tesla's Chris takes FSD 14.3 straight out on its release day. Tesla rewrote the AI compiler using MLIR, claiming a 20% improvement in reaction time and faster model iteration for future point releases. The RL neural network received a broad upgrade. On the drive, the car reads do-not-enter signs and overrides incorrect map data rather than following it blindly, and it avoids a near-miss when another driver cuts across without warning. Still on the to-do list: pothole avoidance and expanded reasoning beyond destination handling.

item.title: The Oil Change Economy: What Maintenance Really Costs Over 100,000 Miles
item.dc:creator: Jacob Hunka
item.description: Everyone knows EVs cost less to maintain. Almost no one has run the full ledger. Oil changes, brake jobs, transmission flushes, spark plugs. Line by line, over 100,000 miles, with real prices from Kelley Blue Book, AAA, Consumer Reports, and the U.S. Department of Energy. The result is not what most people expect.

item.title: The Lucid Gravity Sprints to 100 in 3.6 Seconds, Then Asks If You Would Like a Massage
item.dc:creator: Autogefühl
item.description: Autogefühl takes the Lucid Gravity Grand Touring to Mallorca for a proper test. The numbers are hard to argue with: 123kWh battery, 400kW peak DC charging with 10 to 80% in around 23 minutes, and 3.6 seconds to 100km/h for a car that seats seven adults. Real-world range comes in around 540km (340 miles) at 100km/h and about 450km (280 miles) at 120km/h. The frunk has cup holders and a power socket and enough room to sit inside. The seat massage uses genuine vibration rather than just lumbar pressure and is the best Thomas has tested. The steering needs some fine-tuning. Apple CarPlay is coming. US pricing starts at $80,000 for the entry battery and $95,000 for this Grand Touring.

item.title: Enhauto's 6.4 Update Adds Knight Rider Mode, Muted Reversing, and Track Mode Tesla Forgot to Ship
item.dc:creator: Enhauto
item.description: Enhauto's latest firmware update for its Commander dongle and S3XY accessories adds several features Tesla hasn't built into its own software. Performance Model Y Juniper owners can now enable Track Mode through the S3XY products even though it doesn't appear in Tesla's native menus. Also new: an Actions on Reverse automation that can mute all in-car audio when backing up, a Knight Rider sweeping animation for the ambient light strip, and separate brightness settings for day and night. Brake temperature per wheel is now visible in the S3XY app, a stat Tesla doesn't surface anywhere. All products are 20% off at enhauto.com through April 16.

item.title: China Just Got a Faster Zeekr 007 GT. European Buyers Are Starting Deliveries of the Previous One.
item.dc:creator: AutoSpec Roaming
item.description: AutoSpec Roaming breaks down the updated Zeekr 007 GT just announced in China, right as European deliveries of the 800V version begin this summer. The China-spec car moves to a 900V architecture, adds a 6C-capable NMC battery with 10-80% charging in around 10 minutes, and gains roughly 148 extra horsepower across both motors. The AWD performance version now hits 0-100km/h in 2.84 seconds. Europe gets the 800V model with the LFP Golden Brick battery and around 400kW peak charging, which is still fast but a generation behind. The host argues Zeekr should sync their market releases. The owner forums agree loudly.

item.title: Kia's EV3 Checks a Lot of Boxes for Under $35,000. Then There Are the Door Handles.
item.dc:creator: Consumer Reports
item.description: Consumer Reports gets a first look at the 2027 Kia EV3. The standard 58.3kWh model gets around 220 miles of estimated range. The larger 81.4kWh version stretches that to 320 miles on the front-wheel-drive variant, with AWD available. DC fast charging goes from 10 to 80% in 29 to 31 minutes. Dual 12.3-inch screens, physical climate controls, a wireless charger, and USB charging points built into the rear seatbacks are all welcome. The flush door handles aren't Consumer Reports' favorite, and the rear ones sit high enough that small children may struggle to reach them. Rear seat headroom is snug for anyone over 5'10". On sale late 2026, starting just over $35,000.

item.title: At the New York Auto Show, Nobody Is Going All-In on Electric Anymore
item.dc:creator: Business Today TV
item.description: Business Today TV reports from the 2026 New York International Auto Show, where the mood is notably cautious. US EV sales have slipped from close to 10% of total auto sales to around 6.5%, and the removal of federal purchase incentives has further dampened momentum. Subaru unveiled the Getaway, a three-row electric SUV with over 500km of range, alongside a new Forester Wilderness Hybrid. Kia showed the EV3 alongside the Seltos in gas and hybrid form. Hyundai noted its hybrid lineup is outpacing EV sales. The counterforce: rising fuel prices tied to Middle East tensions are nudging some buyers, especially in California, back toward electric. The industry picture is less a retreat from EVs and more a calculated hedge.

item.title: An FSD First-Timer Takes the Wheel in Downtown LA. Tesla's Vision-Only System Passes Its Toughest Test Yet.
item.dc:creator: Motor Trend
item.description: Motor Trend's Edward Loh hands the keys to Jonny Lieberman, who has never used Tesla FSD, and sends him through one of the most punishing test routes they could design. The 2023 Model Y running FSD v14.2.2 navigates downtown LA traffic, merges onto the world's oldest freeway with about 15 feet of acceleration lane, and climbs the narrow, line-free, storm-damaged back roads of Mount Washington on garbage day. It handles unprotected lefts, pedestrians around blind corners, a pile of rocks, and opposing traffic on a one-lane road. It also hallucinates a ghost and executes three unnecessary three-point turns before recovering. By the Rose Bowl, Jonny has barely touched the steering wheel.

item.title: The AI Energy Crisis Has a Surprising Fix. Turns Out the Data Centers Are the Answer.
item.dc:creator: TED
item.description: Computer scientist Ayse Coskun spent 12 years asking whether data centers could flex their power use to match what the grid needs. The answer is yes. AI facilities run predictable, delayable workloads, making them ideal for soaking up excess solar midday and scaling back during peak demand. Her conductor platform runs on real AI data centers today, cutting power by pausing non-critical jobs without breaking performance promises. During a 2023 Texas heat wave, wholesale electricity prices spiked over 800% in a single afternoon. Flexible data center loads could have blunted that. It is a compelling case that the same technology straining the grid can also help stabilize it.

item.title: Subaru's Smallest EV Is Stylish, Reasonably Priced, and Cruel to Tall Passengers in the Back
item.dc:creator: Consumer Reports
item.description: The 2026 Subaru Uncharted shares its platform with the Toyota C-HR but offers something Toyota doesn't: a front-wheel-drive base trim with 221 horsepower and over 300 miles of claimed range. All-wheel-drive models make 338 horsepower with around 290 miles. NACS port means Superchargers are on the table, and the Lexus-sourced interior feels genuinely upscale for the price. The sloping roofline cuts into cargo depth, the door handles sit high enough to frustrate smaller children, and anyone much over six feet will find their head pressed against the headliner in the rear seat. Good small EV. Just measure your passengers first.

item.title: Tesla Missed Q1 Delivery Targets Again. It Built 50,000 More Cars Than It Sold.
item.dc:creator: CNBC
item.description: Tesla delivered 358,023 vehicles in Q1 2026, missing analyst expectations near 369,000 and producing over 408,000 units in the same period. That 50,000-unit gap between production and deliveries is the figure analysts are watching most closely. Year-over-year deliveries rose 6.3%, but quarter-over-quarter they fell 14.4%, and some analysts now warn of a third consecutive annual decline. Tesla lost the title of world's largest EV maker to BYD last year. Bright spots include recovery in key European markets and China sales up 23%. Shares fell around 5% after the report.

item.title: How Much Solar Could Your Roof Actually Generate? This Free Tool Gives You a Real Answer.
item.dc:creator: National Laboratory of the Rockies
item.description: PVWatts is a free calculator from the National Laboratory of the Rockies that estimates annual and monthly electricity output for a rooftop solar system at any address in the world. Enter your address, pick a system size, and get production numbers based on real local weather data. It is the same tool used by solar installers and researchers. If you have ever wondered whether going solar pencils out for your home, start here.

item.title: Two Wheels, Zero Gas, Room for the Groceries. Cargo E-Bikes Are Having a Moment.
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The Verge's transportation editor Andrew Hawkins tests three electric cargo bikes, rides through Brooklyn with Propel Bikes owner Cat Strain, and checks in on the bike bus movement his own town in Montclair, New Jersey helped pioneer. 234 US cities now score above the bikeability tipping point, up from just 33 in 2019. Cargo e-bikes are outselling traditional bikes. They cost a fraction of a car. But tariffs, slashed bike lane funding, and safety concerns on shared roads are real obstacles. The question is whether the momentum survives the politics. Hawkins makes a strong case that it should.

item.title: IONNA Just Hit 100 Charging Sites. They Built the Whole Network From Scratch in Two Years.
item.dc:creator: Motor Trend
item.description: Motor Trend's Inevitable podcast sits down with IONNA CEO Seth Cutler, who started as employee number one in an empty Honda office in February 2024. Two years later, the eight-automaker joint venture has crossed 100 sites with canopies, bathrooms, and Amazon Just Walk Out stores. No app required. Just plug in or tap your credit card. Discount charging programs are rolling out now for all eight partner brands. Their first site? A 100-year-old gas station where crews found a buried fuel tank during construction. They got it live in nine months anyway.

item.title: LFP or NMC? The Battery Decision Most EV Buyers Never Think About Until It's Too Late.
item.dc:creator: Battery Bound
item.description: Most people hear "LFP lasts longer" and stop there. This video breaks down why that is only part of the story. NMC holds up better in cold weather. LFP can be charged to 100% daily without stress. And an LFP car with 420 km of rated range charged to full may give you more usable daily range than a 500 km NMC car you keep between 20 and 80 percent. Factor in insurance risk, replacement costs, and resale psychology, and the "best" battery depends entirely on how you actually use your car.

item.title: Sodium-Ion Batteries Were Supposed to Be the Budget Option. Two New Studies Say Hold On a Second.
item.dc:creator: Just Have a Think
item.description: Everybody assumed sodium-ion could never compete with lithium on performance because sodium ions are bigger, heavier, and slower. Turns out the ions were fine. The electrodes were the bottleneck. Research from Tokyo University of Science and Germany's Federal Institute for Materials Research shows that fixing how hard carbon anodes are built lets sodium charge at rates comparable to lithium in graphite. CATL is already putting sodium-ion cells in passenger cars. BYD is accelerating development. The chemistry was always capable. We just needed better engineering around it.
